examples.title: Virtual Function Demonstration
examples.description: Program demonstrating runtime polymorphism using virtual functions
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates runtime polymorphism. The base class pointer 'ptr' holds address of derived object. When show() is called, late binding resolves it to Derived::show() at runtime using VTABLE lookup. Virtual destructor ensures proper destruction order.

examples.title: Early Binding Example - Function Overloading
examples.description: Demonstrates early binding through function overloading
examples.explanation: Function overloading uses early binding. The compiler determines which function to call based on argument types at compile time. No runtime overhead. The function address is fixed when the program is compiled.

examples.title: Abstract Class with Pure Virtual Function
examples.description: Shows abstract class and pure virtual function usage
examples.explanation: Shape is an abstract class with pure virtual function draw(). Cannot be instantiated. Derived classes Circle and Rectangle override draw(). Through base class pointers, draw() is resolved at runtime showing runtime polymorphism.

examples.title: Object Slicing Demonstration
examples.description: Shows object slicing and how to prevent it
examples.explanation: When Derived object 'd' is assigned to Base object 'b' by value, object slicing occurs - the 'y' member and derived class behavior are lost. Using pointer 'ptr' preserves the full object and enables polymorphism through late binding.

examples.title: Virtual Destructor Example
examples.description: Demonstrates importance of virtual destructor in polymorphic deletion
examples.explanation: With virtual destructor, when 'ptr' (Base*) is deleted, first Derived destructor runs (frees allocated memory), then Base destructor runs. Without virtual destructor, only Base destructor would be called, causing memory leak.
